_**Agent (default)**_


Here’s what was implemented:

### 1. Dark system navigation (Android + iOS)

- **Android** (`android/app/src/main/res/values/styles.xml`, `values/colors.xml`): `AppTheme` now sets `android:navigationBarColor` to the same dark surface as the app (`#131313`) and `android:windowLightNavigationBar` to `false` so the bar stays **dark with light controls** (replacing the previous light-style bar when the device is in light mode).
- **iOS** (`ios/streamlist/Info.plist`): added `UIUserInterfaceStyle` = `Dark` so system UI (including home indicator / chrome behavior) follows a **dark** style and matches your dark UI.

### 2. App / launcher icon = global app bar icon

The global bar uses **MaterialIcons `movie-filter`** in **`primary_container`** (`StreamListLogo.tsx`). The **Android adaptive icon** (API 26+) now uses that same glyph via `ic_launcher_foreground.xml` on `ic_launcher_background` (`#131313`).

For **older Android densities** and **all iOS sizes**, a small generator script rasterizes that exact glyph from `MaterialIcons.ttf` (same font as the app):

- `scripts/generate_app_icons.py` — run after font/branding changes to refresh PNGs.
- Regenerated **`mipmap-*/ic_launcher.png`** and **`ic_launcher_round.png`**.
- Added full **iOS App Icon** set under `ios/streamlist/Images.xcassets/AppIcon.appiconset/` and wired filenames in **`Contents.json`**.

### Files touched

| Area | Files |
|------|--------|
| Android theme / nav bar | `android/app/src/main/res/values/styles.xml`, `values/colors.xml` |
| Adaptive launcher | `drawable/ic_launcher_foreground.xml`, `mipmap-anydpi-v26/ic_launcher.xml`, `ic_launcher_round.xml` |
| iOS | `Info.plist`, `Images.xcassets/AppIcon.appiconset/Contents.json` + new PNGs |
| Regeneration | `scripts/generate_app_icons.py` |
| Raster icons | All `mipmap-*` launcher PNGs; `AppIcon.appiconset/*.png` |

Gradle wasn’t run here (wrapper couldn’t download in this environment), so run `./gradlew assembleDebug` locally to confirm the Android resource merge.

If you instead wanted the **in-app** header to show **only** the movie icon (no “Streamlist” text), say so and we can adjust `StreamListLogo` or `App.tsx` only.

---

_**User**_

please check the screenshot attached, change the current app logo to this.

Here’s what was updated to use your clapperboard logo everywhere.

### In-app (global app bar + error screen)
- **`src/components/StreamListLogo.tsx`** — Replaced the Material `movie-filter` icon with an **`Image`** that loads `src/assets/images/app_logo.png`, keeping the same height as before (`spacing.xl - 6`) and **aspect ratio 54:58**.
- **`src/assets/images/`** — Holds the logo plus **`app_logo@2x.png`** and **`app_logo@3x.png`** (generated for sharp scaling on high-DPI screens).
- **`types/assets.d.ts`** — Declares `*.png` for `require()`, and **`tsconfig.json`** includes it.

### Android & iOS launcher icons
- **`scripts/generate_app_icons.py`** — Now builds all launcher and App Store sizes from `app_logo.png`: centered on the same **`#131313`** background as your theme (~72% of the square so it matches adaptive safe zones).
- Regenerated **`mipmap-*/ic_launcher.png`**, **`ic_launcher_round.png`**, **`drawable/ic_launcher_foreground.png`** (adaptive foreground), and **iOS `AppIcon.appiconset` PNGs**.
- Removed the old vector **`ic_launcher_foreground.xml`** so the adaptive icon uses the new bitmap.

### Source asset
- Your file is stored as **`Phase 2/streamlist/src/assets/images/app_logo.png`** (copied from the screenshot you attached).

To refresh icons after changing the master asset, update `app_logo.png` and run:

`python3 scripts/generate_app_icons.py` from the `streamlist` folder.

All **33 Jest tests** passed after these changes.

---

_**User**_

change the app icon into movie filter icon which is in existing global app bar @streamlist/App.tsx . reert the app logo changes on global app bar.

---
